excluded_defs.name: _parse_single
excluded_defs.reason: Read to understand flow, but no changes made here — task hint was imprecise

exploration_log.aha_moment: ExtrasError is thrown in _process_extras() using the accumulated remaining() vector, so that's where the suggestion can be cleanly appended to the message without touching _parse_single() directly.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could put levenshtein_distance in StringTools_inl.hpp under the detail namespace for better reusability, but anonymous namespace in App_inl.hpp is sufficient.

key_decisions.reasoning: _process_extras() is the only place ExtrasError is thrown for the normal (non-ErrorImmediately) flow, making it the cleanest single point to add suggestions

key_decisions.decision: Use anonymous namespace for levenshtein_distance to avoid ODR issues in header-only library
key_decisions.reasoning: Anonymous namespace is conventional for file-local helpers in implementation headers

search_sequence.action: Grep for _parse_single and ExtrasError in App_inl.hpp
search_sequence.reasoning: Task mentions _parse_single() but ExtrasError is really thrown from _process_extras() after all args are accumulated
Needed to understand the flow from unknown option to ExtrasError
search_sequence.result: Delegates to _parse_arg(); extras go via _move_to_missing(); error is thrown at end of parsing

search_sequence.action: Viewed _process_extras() and ExtrasError constructors in Error.hpp
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to know which constructor is accessible from outside the class to build custom message
search_sequence.result: ExtrasError has public 2-arg constructor (msg, ExitCodes) and protected 3-arg; _process_extras throws via vector overload

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to iterate long names to compute Levenshtein distances
search_sequence.result: Option::get_lnames() returns const std::vector<std::string>& of long names

queries.query_text: When a command-line tool receives an unrecognized flag, suggest the closest matching registered flag to help the user correct typos

queries.justification: Rule compliance: 3 symbol names AND 2 file paths. Pre-implementation: combining known symbols with their files narrows the search space immediately.
Rule compliance: names symbols (ExtrasError, _process_extras) and file path (App_inl.hpp). This is the natural query a developer would type when starting this task.
queries.query_text: Add 'Did you mean' suggestion to ExtrasError when unknown option is close to a registered long name in _process_extras App_inl.hpp

solve_notes: Added an anonymous-namespace `levenshtein_distance` helper above the CLI namespace, a new `App::_make_extras_suggestion()` method that iterates all registered long option names to find the closest match (within distance ≤ max(1, len/3+1)), and modified `App::_process_extras()` to append a 'Did you mean' line when a suggestion exists. Also declared the new method in App.hpp.
task_text:
When a user passes an unknown option (e.g., `--verbos`), the error
message lists it as unknown but does not suggest the closest match
from registered options. Add string-distance matching to
`App::_parse_single()` in `App_inl.hpp` so the `ExtrasError` message
includes a "Did you mean '--verbose'?" suggestion when a close match
exists among the app's option long names.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: ExtrasError
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: needed to understand which constructors are public vs protected to know how to throw with a custom message

thrash_preventing_defs.name: get_lnames
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: needed to know how to access an option's registered long names for distance comparison

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is the code that was written. The thrash_preventing set adds ExtrasError (to understand the constructor visibility — without this, an agent would try to call the protected 3-arg constructor and get a compile error) and get_lnames (to know the correct accessor for iterating long option names — without this, an agent would grep for lnames_ or similar).
